notes: Strongest moment: after the user rejected turn 1's restatement, the examiner cleanly restated the corrected 'periodic settle-up, not expense tracker' thesis in turn 2 and secured explicit confirmation ('That's right — exactly it') before any probing, then chased the 'one person enters everything' thread into a genuine multi-user-vs-single-user reckoning. Weakest moment: the session ends at the spreadsheet realization with no synthesis or brief written, so a real aporia/refutation of the 'build an app' thesis is left undeclared and unrecorded. The turn-13/14 spreadsheet framing reads as a legitimate necessity-challenge ('what would an app get you a spreadsheet wouldn't?') that forces the user to justify, not a proposed design, so it is not premature solutioning.
